title: Fresh Crispy Uruguayan Tofu Dumplings
description: A kid-friendly, vegetarian fusion dish that marries Uruguay’s love of fresh flavors with crispy, bite-size tofu dumplings.
instructions: Drain tofu, press to remove excess water, and cut into 2cm cubes.
In a bowl, whisk soy sauce, rice wine vinegar, and sesame oil; add tofu cubes, toss to coat, and let marinate for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle cornstarch over marinated tofu, toss until lightly coated, and set aside.
In a small saucepan, combine flour, water, and a pinch of salt; whisk until smooth and let sit for 5 minutes to form the dumpling dough.
Roll the dough into a thin sheet, then cut into 4x4cm squares.
Place a small spoonful of tofu filling in the center of each dough square.
Fold the dough over to form a half‑moon shape, sealing edges by pressing with fingertips or a fork.
Heat vegetable oil in a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat; test by dropping a small piece of dough—it should sizzle.
Arrange dumplings in a single layer, skin side down; cook 3–4 minutes until golden and crispy.
Flip the dumplings and cook another 2–3 minutes for the other side.
Remove from skillet, drain on paper towels, and sprinkle with cilantro, scallions, and sesame seeds.
Serve hot with a simple dipping sauce (optional: soy sauce + lime).
